name: Upsonic
tagline: Build autonomous and traditional AI agents in Python
seoTitle: Upsonic - Python Framework for Autonomous AI Agents
metaDescription: Upsonic is an open-source Python framework for building autonomous and traditional AI agents with custom tools, sandboxed workspaces, and OCR support.
overview: Upsonic is an open-source Python framework for building AI agents. It supports both autonomous agents, which run multi-step tasks on their own, and more traditional agents that you drive task by task. You describe what you want as a Task and hand it to an Agent, which calls a language model and any tools you give it.

It is aimed at Python developers who want to wire agents into real workflows without building the orchestration plumbing themselves. Autonomous agents restrict file and shell operations to a defined workspace, blocking path traversal and dangerous commands, which makes them safer to point at log analysis or other system tasks.

As a general agent framework, Upsonic covers the common building blocks: model selection, custom tools, MCP integration, prebuilt community agents, and an optional OCR pipeline for pulling text out of PDFs and images.
features: Two agent styles: AutonomousAgent for self-directed multi-step tasks and Agent for step-by-step traditional workflows
Custom tools defined with a simple @tool decorator on typed Python functions
Sandboxed workspaces for autonomous agents that block path traversal and dangerous shell commands
Prebuilt, community-contributed autonomous agents that run from install in seconds
MCP tools integration to connect agents to external data sources and services
Optional OCR pipeline with multiple engines (EasyOCR, RapidOCR, Tesseract, PaddleOCR, DeepSeek OCR)

gettingStarted.intro: Install Upsonic, then run either an autonomous or a traditional agent against a task.

steps.heading: Install Upsonic
steps.body: Install the package with uv (or pip).
steps.code:
uv pip install upsonic
# pip install upsonic

steps.heading: Run a traditional agent
steps.body: Create an Agent with a model, wrap your instruction in a Task, and call print_do.
steps.code:
from upsonic import Agent, Task

agent = Agent(model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5", name="Stock Analyst Agent")

task = Task(description="Analyze the current market trends")

agent.print_do(task)

steps.heading: Add a custom tool
steps.body: Decorate a typed function with @tool and pass it to a Task via the tools argument.
steps.code:
from upsonic import Agent, Task
from upsonic.tools import tool

@tool
def sum_tool(a: float, b: float) -> float:
    """
    Add two numbers together.

    Args:
        a: First number
        b: Second number

    Returns:
        The sum of a and b
    """
    return a + b

task = Task(
    description="Calculate 15 + 27",
    tools=[sum_tool]
)

agent = Agent(model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5", name="Calculator Agent")

result = agent.print_do(task)

steps.heading: Run an autonomous agent
steps.body: Give an AutonomousAgent a model and a workspace; file and shell operations stay restricted to that workspace.
steps.code:
from upsonic import AutonomousAgent, Task

agent = AutonomousAgent(
    model="anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5",
    workspace="/path/to/logs"
)

task = Task("Analyze server logs and detect anomaly patterns")

agent.print_do(task)

useCases: Run an autonomous agent over a workspace to analyze server logs and surface anomaly patterns
Build a task-driven agent that calls your own Python functions as tools
Extract text from scanned PDFs and images using the optional OCR pipeline
Connect agents to external services and data sources through MCP tools
